origin: balance case (expected PASS) -- a load whose lower-than-CSV count is fully explained by a documented suppression and re-queried, distinct from the clean-load good
artifact: A cold-email setup report reads: 'PASS. CSV had 80 rows; suppression removed 12 already-contacted addresses (list shown). Re-queried the live campaign after load: 68 distinct members. 68 = 80 - 12, reconciled. firstName non-empty on all 68 loaded rows; Touch-1 preview to the first lead renders "Hi Dana," cleanly.'
rubric: The loaded count (68) is lower than the CSV (80), which superficially looks like a partial load, but the gap is fully reconciled by a documented 12-row suppression and the final 68 was re-queried from the live campaign, with required fields validated and the first send rendering cleanly. A correct verifier must PASS it. Failing it because 68 < 80 is over-rejection of a re-queried, reconciled load.
